# meta-toolkit

> Zero-dependency JavaScript toolkit for meta programming and OOP: name-casing conversions, property descriptor manipulation, aliases, prototype traversal, iterators, deep path access, option merging, and comparator adapters.

- NPM: https://npmjs.org/package/meta-toolkit
- GitHub: https://github.com/uhop/meta-toolkit
- Wiki: https://github.com/uhop/meta-toolkit/wiki
- License: BSD-3-Clause

## Install

```bash
npm install meta-toolkit
```

## Quick start

```js
import {fromKebabCase, toCamelCase, toPascalCase} from 'meta-toolkit/names.js';

const parts = fromKebabCase('foo-bar-baz');
console.log(toCamelCase(parts));  // 'fooBarBaz'
console.log(toPascalCase(parts)); // 'FooBarBaz'
```

```js
import {addAliases} from 'meta-toolkit/aliases.js';

class Foo {
  greet() { return 'hello'; }
}
addAliases(Foo.prototype, {greet: 'hi, sayHello'});

new Foo().hi(); // 'hello'
```

```js
import {get, set, forceSet} from 'meta-toolkit/path.js';

const obj = {};
forceSet(obj, 'a.b.c', 42); // obj = {a: {b: {c: 42}}}
get(obj, 'a.b.c');           // 42
```

## Modules

### Names (`meta-toolkit/names.js`)

Name-casing conversions between compound name formats.

- **capitalize(name)** — capitalize first letter, lowercase rest.
- **fromCamelCase(name)** / **toCamelCase(names)** — split/join camelCase.
- **fromPascalCase(name)** / **toPascalCase(names)** — split/join PascalCase.
- **fromSnakeCase(name)** / **toSnakeCase(names)** — split/join snake_case.
- **toAllCapsSnakeCase(names)** — join as UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.
- **fromKebabCase(name)** / **toKebabCase(names)** — split/join kebab-case.
- **fromWords(name)** / **toWords(names)** — split/join whitespace-separated words (join is lowercase).
- **toTitleCase(names)** — join as space-separated capitalized words.

### Descriptors (`meta-toolkit/descriptors.js`)

Property descriptor creation and manipulation.

- **defaultDescriptor** — the default descriptor template `{configurable: true, enumerable: false}`.
- **makeGetter(getter, initDescriptor?)** — create a getter descriptor.
- **makeSetter(setter, initDescriptor?)** — create a setter descriptor.
- **makeAccessors(getter, setter, initDescriptor?)** — create a getter+setter descriptor.
- **addDescriptor(target, names, descriptor, force?)** — define a descriptor on target.
- **addDescriptors(target, dict, force?)** — define multiple descriptors from a dictionary.
- **addAccessor(target, names, getter, setter, force?)** — add a getter+setter.
- **addAccessors(target, dict, force?)** — add multiple accessors from a dictionary of `{get, set}` pairs.
- **addGetter(target, names, getter, force?)** — add a single getter.
- **addGetters(target, dict, force?)** — add multiple getters from a dictionary.
- **addSetter(target, names, setter, force?)** — add a single setter.
- **addSetters(target, dict, force?)** — add multiple setters from a dictionary.
- **addProtoDescriptor/addProtoDescriptors/addProtoAccessor/addProtoAccessors/addProtoGetter/addProtoGetters/addProtoSetter/addProtoSetters(Class, ...)** — the same installers targeting `Class.prototype` (sugar).
- **copyDescriptors(target, source, names, force?)** — copy descriptors between objects.

### Aliases (`meta-toolkit/aliases.js`)

Property aliasing built on descriptors.

- **addAlias(object, name, aliases, force?)** — alias a single property.
- **addAliases(object, dict, force?)** — alias multiple properties via a dictionary.
- **addProtoAlias(Class, name, aliases, force?)** — alias a property on `Class.prototype` (sugar).
- **addProtoAliases(Class, dict, force?)** — alias multiple properties on `Class.prototype` (sugar).

### Iterators (`meta-toolkit/iterators.js`)

Iterator augmentation and transformation helpers.

- **augmentIterator(iterator)** — add `[Symbol.iterator]` if missing.
- **normalizeIterator(iterator)** — normalize using `Iterator.from()` when available, otherwise `augmentIterator`. On modern runtimes the result exposes the full native helper family (`take`, `drop`, `flatMap`, …).
- **mapIterator(iterable, callbackFn)** — lazy map over an iterable or iterator; single-use result, forwards `return()`.
- **filterIterator(iterable, callbackFn)** — lazy filter over an iterable or iterator; single-use result, forwards `return()`.

### Prototypes (`meta-toolkit/prototypes.js`)

Prototype chain inspection.

- **prototypes(object, skipSelf?)** — generator yielding each prototype up to (excluding) `Object.prototype`.
- **getPropertyDescriptor(object, name)** — like `getOwnPropertyDescriptor` but walks the prototype chain.

### Path (`meta-toolkit/path.js`)

Deep property access by dot-delimited path or array of keys.

- **get(object, path, options?)** — get a nested value.
- **has(object, path, options?)** — check that every path segment exists (distinguishes a missing path from an `undefined` value).
- **set(object, path, value, options?)** — set a nested value (intermediate segments must exist; the final one is created).
- **forceSet(object, path, value, options?)** — set a nested value, creating intermediate objects as needed.
- **remove(object, path, options?)** — delete a nested property.

Options: `{delimiter, defaultValue}`.

**Path safety**: these functions walk user-supplied keys without sanitizing magic property names
(`__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`). If you pass externally-sourced paths, validate at the
application boundary first — the library does not.

### Options (`meta-toolkit/options.js`)

Constructor option merging.

- **copyOptions(target, defaults, ...sources)** — merge defaults then sources into target. Only keys present in `defaults` are copied from sources.

### Comparators (`meta-toolkit/comparators.js`)

Adapters between `less`, `compare`, and `equal` function styles.

- **compareFromLess(lessFn)** — adapt `(a,b) => bool` to `(a,b) => number`.
- **lessFromCompare(compareFn)** — adapt `(a,b) => number` to `(a,b) => bool`.
- **equalFromLess(lessFn)** — derive equality from a less function.
- **equalFromCompare(compareFn)** — derive equality from a compare function.
- **reverseLess(lessFn)** — reverse a less function.
- **reverseCompare(compareFn)** — reverse a compare function.

## Key concepts

- **ESM-only** — `"type": "module"`.
- **Zero dependencies** — no runtime deps; only devDependencies for testing.
- **TypeScript** — hand-written `.d.ts` declarations for all modules.
- **Subpath exports** — import via `meta-toolkit/<module>.js` (wildcard `./*` → `./src/*`).

## Links

- Docs: https://github.com/uhop/meta-toolkit/wiki
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/meta-toolkit
- Full LLM reference: https://github.com/uhop/meta-toolkit/blob/master/llms-full.txt
